video_title: FERC FAST-TRACKS GRID RULES FOR AI DATA CENTERS
episode_headline: The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission launched a fast-track proceeding on June 18 to standardize how large power users, including data centers, connect to the U.S. transmission grid.
story_angle: The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission launched a fast-track proceeding on June 18 to standardize how large power users, including data centers, connect to the U.S. transmission grid.
stakes: FERC ordered regional grid operators to submit tariff revisions for large load interconnections within 30 days or explain within 60 days why existing rules are sufficient.
curiosity_gap: Can infrastructure keep up with AI demand?
